author: @maherhasna4979
text:
Sir, Thank you for you easy and simplified videos but still very beneficial. I feel that I have many many things in common with you.
Once again. Thank you indeed.

author: @B.Cote39
text: This type of setup has been on my mind for years !!! Glad to see it working !!! Thx Robert

author: @SimonPlatten
text: Is there any chance of posting a parts list on where to get the parts and an instruction / guide on how to build the gear box?

author: @tom8437
text: How much pedaling would it take to charge devices with a setup like that? I would love to be able to exercise and generate power at the same time, and it would be great in the event of a grid failure.

author: @Gwydion67
text:
More than some Milliwatt, I'm really surprised! 😲...🤣👍
(Especially from such a tiny thing as a printer motor.)
Makes me seriously curious about the real world output of a formerly-vac-motor generator. If the printer-type is awsome, the vac-type must be INCREDIBLE! ⚡😱⚡

author: @make425
text: If you don't have a 💯 free Energy machine there in your shop somewhere there's something wrong.

author: @hanslepoeter5167
text: Some change of plans ! But you're right. The universal motor could do well at high rpm. I wonder, when you put a magnet near the stator will it have a predictable polarity ? Maybe ....

author: @lightcapmath2777
text: What type of adhesive did you use to glue the rubber to the metal wheel?

author: @Buzzhumma
text: it would be great to see your take on an electric eal! They have one in a university generating 400 watts to power lighting.Maybe a mechanical version of the same vibrating plates!

author: @Buzzhumma
text: thumbs up.

author: @lnwolf41
text: that was pretty good for a LED light, how about a 100 watt incandescent light bulb?

author: @wongowonga
text: I'd love to see how much energy you could harness from a hamster in a wheel! It's so cliché that it would be fun to see exactly what a hamster could do! If you don't have one, maybe you could borrow a friends. Maybe the future of energy is hamster farms! I could imagine a video with that content attracting a big viewing audience too... maybe a future project?

author: @ThomasAndersonbsf
text: you are turning up a lot of printer parts, and some look like laser printers, so I am wondering if you have torn down the bits that have the rollers and fusers and found any t hat have soft squishy rubber coatings on them, as I have a couple that feel and react like urethane rubber, (is an orange-ish red color) and really thinking of making a van de graaff generator using them and some large solid teflon block I have access to,

author: @actudoran
text: Super stuff

author: @clive280178
text: Another brilliant vid, love the recycling and cross engineering. So inspiring, cheers rob. Do you need any labourers haha

author: @captainprototype187
text:
didnt know there was such a small beautiful motor underneath that big casing of the vac motor. I have plenty of
them and they  take up  a lot of space.

author: @MrAnderson4509
text: I need to find one of those exercise bike drivelines, it looks like an air raid siren waiting to be brought to life... thanks again Robert for all the ideas you always find great little bits to play around with, unfortunately the scrapper seems to get to a lot of things that could be repurposed, Around me. Somehow I need to get faster because I often just fine empty carcasses stripped of the Almighty golden copper, tweakers are a pain. Thank you again for all your great videos, I really appreciate your hard work

author: @danielade9694
text: Probably going to junk yard or salvage yard would find a lot of the same things as well easily and for very little money!

author: @danielade9694
text: You work with what you have! That's the whole point in DIY use what you have around you

author: @samuelthomasengardio
text: Harbor freight sells a 10KW tractor PTO generator for about $320 USD. Optimal RPMs at the Rotor is 1,000. This is the cheapest and most efficient way that I've found to approach larger projects. You can always grab a cheap 1:30 and reverse it to the Rotor shaft, after that, torque is king.

replies.author: @samuelthomasengardio
replies.text: Gears are terribly expensive globally, sprocket and chain ratios will get you where you want to go without the cost.

author: @murb2586
text:
loving the self-empowerment and diy angle.  do you think youd be able to create a radio transceiver, for diy ham radio communication technology.  i feel like youre the guy to ask.

thanks so much for your selfless efforts as always, all who do good have support whether they know it or not, you sir are supported.

peace and free electrons to all

author: @Kapalek84
text: I think this generator might be quite efficient. It would be very interesting to know what is the efficiency of this device! Great idea with the tooth belt, it is a great gearing solution :D Cheers!

author: @kenrowe167
text: Hi Rob. I think your gear idea is the cleverest thing I've seen in a long while. But what do you do if the belt doesn't fit perfectly around the circumference (half a tooth too long or too short)? Is it just serendipity that yours seem to fit perfectly, or dit you have to turn the centre parts down a bit of the lathe? Or am I missing something obvious?

replies.author: @tremail564
replies.text: I'll go bigger,by adding a layer under the belt to make up the size difference. It wouldn't have to be big because your changing the radius squared compared to circumference .

author: @richardcoppack5357
text: Awesome is the right word.

author: @AM-us7cz
text: Bloody Brilliant 👏. Thanks so much Robert this is just what I was looking for. ( Why didn't I think of that?) Simplistically at it's best. Cheers mate 👍

author: @scantrain5007
text: The gearbox for the generator is very intersting. I don't see much potential in the printer-motor as generator ... see more in the hoover motor: Put on the rotor variable DC to get AC on the stator. So in case connect to a wind turbine it should be possible to get out a stabile voltage to charge batteries. And - off course - keep the flywheel in the system as stabilisator for the voltage because wind can drop and rising fast.

text:

author: @mikewillemse3761
text: reducing the size of your top cog will increase the speed and should therefore increase your output

author: @William_Hada
text: Awesome build! I really like the idea of making gears from those drive belts.

author: @jimmycorkhill1390
text: I think Ive watched nearly all your videos and am sure that this is my favourite. It is so simple, clever and deliverable for so many. The change in motor adds to the video, as it presents an alternative option that are generally readily available. Im thinking that this could be not only a windturbine and obvious bike generator but perhaps best for me, a backup battery charger for low solar days using a small petrol motor, say lawnmower. Then using one of those cheap ebay gas converters to utilize bio gas which I had intended to make this year by converting an IBC into a digestor. (videos on youtube). Best wishes.

author: @dexterdixon2000
text: Good work! I doubt the printer motor would deliver 4 amps for very long without its windings melting! What current does it put into a 12V battery?

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: nah - probably not lol - but then just swap the motor for you motor of choice - the basic idea is what will work I think - as for amps - no idea build one and see - this took me about 3 hours to make start to finish
